coredata.dc:title: Sn-Cu Alloy Materials with Optimized Nanoporous Structure and Enhanced Performance for Lithium-Ion Batteries Prepared by Dealloying
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2012 The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Authors. Published by ESG. Published by Elsevier B.V.
coredata.dc:creator: Tan, Chunhui
Qi, Gongwei
Li, Yeping
Guo, Jing
Wang, Xin
Kong, Delong
Wang, Hongjun
Zhang, Shuyong
coredata.dc:description: Three kinds of binder-free nanoporous Sn-Cu alloys are prepared by electroplating followed by electrochemical dealloying and tested as possible negative electrode materials for lithium-ion battery. The morphology and structure of these alloys were characterized by X-ray diffraction, scanning electron microscopy and atomic absorption spectrophotometer. Experimental results suggest that the composition, pore size, size distribution, homogeneity of the alloys can be facilely controlled by changing the ratio of main salts in the electroplating bath. The Sn content in the alloys after electrochemical dealloying reduces significantly and Cu6Sn5 become the main active component. However there is no obvious decrease in specific capacity. The initial specific capacities of all the materials are in 480-810 mA h g-1 range. But the capacity of as-deposited Sn-Cu alloys remain only 110-190 mA h g-1 after 50 cycles, while one of the capacity of the nanoporous Sn-Cu alloys remain around 400 mA h g-1 after the same cycles. This suggests that both capacity and cycling performance can be improved by optimizing the nanostructure of the materials using electrochemical dealloying.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Lithium-ion battery,
Negative materials,
Sn-Cu alloy,
nanoporous materials
Electrochemical dealloying

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: ACKNOWLEDGEMENTS This work was financially supported by Natural Science Foundation of Shandong Province (Project No. ZR2009BM012) and National Basic Research Program of China (No.2011CB935901)

xocs:doc.xocs:rawtext: Sn-Cu Alloy Materials with Optimized Nanoporous Structure and Enhanced Performance for Lithium-Ion Batteries Prepared by Dealloying Chunhui Tan, 1 Gongwei Qi, 1 Yeping Li, 1 Jing Guo, 1 Xin Wang, 1 Delong Kong, 2 Hongjun Wang, 2 Shuyong Zhang, 1 syzhang@sdu.edu.cn 1 Key Laboratory of Colloid and Interface Chemistry, Ministry of Education, School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Shandong University, Jinan, China, 250100 Key Laboratory of Colloid and Interface Chemistry, Ministry of Education, School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering Shandong University Jinan 250100 China Key Laboratory of Colloid and Interface Chemistry, Ministry of Education, School of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Shandong University, Jinan, China, 250100 2 Shandong Sacred Sun Power Co. Ltd. Qufu, China, 273100 Shandong Sacred Sun Power Co. Ltd. Qufu 273100 China Shandong Sacred Sun Power Co. Ltd. Qufu, China, 273100 Three kinds of binder-free nanoporous Sn-Cu alloys are prepared by electroplating followed by electrochemical dealloying and tested as possible negative electrode materials for lithium-ion battery. The morphology and structure of these alloys were characterized by X-ray diffraction, scanning electron microscopy and atomic absorption spectrophotometer. Experimental results suggest that the composition, pore size, size distribution, homogeneity of the alloys can be facilely controlled by changing the ratio of main salts in the electroplating bath. The Sn content in the alloys after electrochemical dealloying reduces significantly and Cu6 Sn Sn5 become the main active component. However there is no obvious decrease in specific capacity. The initial specific capacities of all the materials are in 480-810 mA h g become the main active component. However there is no obvious decrease in specific capacity. The initial specific capacities of all the materials are in 480-810 mA h g-1 range. But the capacity of as-deposited Sn-Cu alloys remain only 110-190 mA h g range. But the capacity of as-deposited Sn-Cu alloys remain only 110-190 mA h g-1 after 50 cycles, while one of the capacity of the nanoporous Sn-Cu alloys remain around 400 mA h g after 50 cycles, while one of the capacity of the nanoporous Sn-Cu alloys remain around 400 mA h g-1 after the same cycles. This suggests that both capacity and cycling performance can be improved by optimizing the nanostructure of the materials using electrochemical dealloying.Keywords Lithium-ion battery, Negative materials, Sn-Cu alloy, nanoporous materials Electrochemical dealloying References 1. M. Armand, J.M. Tarascon, Nature 451: (2008) 652-M. Armand and J.M. Tarascon, Nature, 451 (2008) 652. 2. M. Winter, J.O. Besenhard, Electrochimi Acta 45: (1999) 31-M. Winter and J.O. Besenhard, Electrochimi Acta, 45 (1999) 31. 3. A. Sivashanmugam, T.P. Kumar, N.G. Renganathan, S. Gopukumar, M. Wohlfahrt-Mehrens, J. Garche, J. Power Sources 144: (2005) 197-A. Sivashanmugam, T.P. Kumar, N.G. Renganathan, S. Gopukumar, M. Wohlfahrt-Mehrens and J. Garche, J. Power Sources, 144 (2005) 197. 4. K. Ui, S. Kikuchi, Y. Kadoma, N. Kumagai, S. Ito, J. Power Sources 189: (2009) 224-K. Ui, S. Kikuchi, Y. Kadoma, N. Kumagai and S. Ito, J. Power Sources, 189 (2009) 224. 5. L. Huang, H.B. Wei, F.S. Ke, X.Y. Fan, J.T. Li, S.G. Sun, Electrochim. Acta 54: (2009) 2693-L. Huang, H.B. Wei, F. Yang and S. Sun, Electrochem. Commun., 9 (2007) 228. 7. N. Tamura, R. Ohshita, M. Fujimoto, S. Fujitani, M. Kamino, J. Power Sources 107: (2002) 48-N. Tamura, R. Ohshita, M. Fujimoto, S. Fujitani and M. Kamino, J. Power Sources, 107 (2002) 48. 8. J.C. Lytle, H. Yan, N.S. Ergang, W.H. Smyrl, A. Stein, J. Mater. Chem. 14: (2004) 1616-J.C. Lytle, H. Yan, N.S. Ergang, W.H. Smyrl and A. Stein, J. Mater. Chem., 14 (2004) 1616. 9. Z. Chen, J. Qian, X. Ai, Y. Cao, H. Yang, J. Power Sources 189: (2009) 730-Z. Chen, J. Qian, X. Ai, Y. Cao and H. Yang, J. Amatucci, Solid State Ionics, 167 (2004) 29 e nanoporous Sn-Cu alloys remain around 400 mA h g-1 after the same cycles. This suggests that both capacity and cycling performance can be improved by optimizing the nanostructure of the materials using electrochemical dealloying.Keywords Lithium-ion battery, Negative materials, Sn-Cu alloy, nanoporous materials Electrochemical dealloying References 1. simple-head.ce:title: Sn-Cu Alloy Materials with Optimized Nanoporous Structure and Enhanced Performance for Lithium-Ion Batteries Prepared by Dealloying

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Three kinds of binder-free nanoporous Sn-Cu alloys are prepared by electroplating followed by electrochemical dealloying and tested as possible negative electrode materials for lithium-ion battery. The morphology and structure of these alloys were characterized by X-ray diffraction, scanning electron microscopy and atomic absorption spectrophotometer. Experimental results suggest that the composition, pore size, size distribution, homogeneity of the alloys can be facilely controlled by changing the ratio of main salts in the electroplating bath. The Sn content in the alloys after electrochemical dealloying reduces significantly and Cu6Sn5 become the main active component. However there is no obvious decrease in specific capacity. The initial specific capacities of all the materials are in 480-810 mA h g-1 range. But the capacity of as-deposited Sn-Cu alloys remain only 110-190 mA h g-1 after 50 cycles, while one of the capacity of the nanoporous Sn-Cu alloys remain around 400 mA h g-1 after the same cycles. This suggests that both capacity and cycling performance can be improved by optimizing the nanostructure of the materials using electrochemical dealloying.
